name: cutie-product
description: Your AI assistant that controls your PC with love. Optional unlimited autonomy runs until you stop or the API refuses (credits/quota); otherwise local step and time limits apply.

cutie-product.baseApiUrl.description: Base URL for the hosted Xpersona API.

cutie-product.binary.baseApiUrl.description: Optional separate base URL for Cutie's prompt-to-app API endpoints. When empty, uses cutie-product.baseApiUrl.

cutie-product.binary.runtime.description: Hosted OpenHands-backed runtime for Cutie chat. Legacy local runtimes are debug-only and not used by the standard chat flow.

cutie-product.binary.qwen.model.description: Model name for Qwen Code when binary.runtime is qwenCode.

cutie-product.binary.qwen.baseUrl.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL for Qwen. When empty, defaults to {baseApiUrl}/api/v1/hf.

cutie-product.binary.qwen.executable.description: Optional path to Qwen CLI. Leave empty to use the SDK-bundled CLI.

cutie-product.binary.qwen.cliWrapper.description: Use CLI wrapper to reduce path leak into model context (experimental).

cutie-product.model.description: Model id for hosted playground assist (HF Router id, e.g. moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5:fastest, openai/gpt-oss-120b:groq) or registry alias (playground-default, qwen-coder-32b). Requires HF_TOKEN on the server.

cutie-product.reasoningLevel.description: Reasoning depth for Cutie chat (stored for the workspace; API wiring may follow in later builds).

cutie-product.promptMarkdownPath.description: Optional workspace-relative or absolute markdown file that defines Cutie's observable operating style. When it cannot be loaded, Cutie falls back to the built-in runtime prompt.

cutie-product.experimentalDesktopAdapter.description: Enable experimental desktop automation adapters on macOS and Linux.

cutie-product.showDiffToast.description: Show an information toast when Cutie updates a workspace file (in-chat diff is always recorded).

cutie-product.autoOpenDiff.description: Open the side-by-side diff editor when Cutie edits a file. Off by default so your current editor stays focused; the in-chat diff and “Open in editor” still work.

cutie-product.contextPreviewChars.description: Maximum characters of the active editor buffer (or selection) included in Cutie live context.

cutie-product.openFilePreviewLines.description: First N lines of each open tab to include as a preview in context (0 disables tab previews).

cutie-product.maxOpenFilesInContext.description: How many open editor tabs to list in live context metadata and previews.

cutie-product.maxToolsPerBatch.description: Maximum tool calls Cutie may run from a single planning response (observe tools plus optional one mutation last).

cutie-product.contextReceiptWindow.description: How many recent tool receipts are included in live context JSON.

cutie-product.investigationPreflight.description: On workspace investigation runs, run a cheap git_status + list_files once before the first model planning turn and inject summaries into context.

cutie-product.objectiveBasedRuns.description: For code tasks, decompose the user request into a checklist and require a structured final that marks every objective done or blocked before the run completes.

cutie-product.objectiveBasedInvestigatio.description: When enabled, workspace investigation runs also use objective checklists (in addition to code_change).

cutie-product.maxToolSteps.description: Maximum tool-call steps per run (planning waves count tools executed). Edit/mutation repair attempts and objective-checklist JSON repair budget scale with this value (unless Unlimited autonomy is on).

cutie-product.maxWorkspaceMutations.description: Maximum successful workspace mutations (edit/write/mkdir/run_command) per run.

cutie-product.unlimitedAutonomy.description: When enabled, Cutie disables the local 10-minute wall clock and the max tool-step / workspace-mutation caps (uses more model calls). Edit/mutation repairs and objective JSON repairs both use a higher but finite cap (256). The run still stops when you cancel, when the model finishes normally (final JSON / objectives), or when the hosted API returns an error (credits, quota, auth, rate limit).

scripts.test:cutie-native: npm run compile && node --test tests/binary-sse.test.cjs tests/cutie-file-patch.test.cjs tests/cutie-model-protocol.test.cjs tests/cutie-model-capabilities.test.cjs tests/cutie-operating-prompt.test.cjs tests/cutie-native-autonomy.test.cjs tests/cutie-autonomy-controller.test.cjs tests/cutie-code-intelligence.test.cjs tests/cutie-edit-synthesis.test.cjs tests/cutie-runtime-mutation.test.cjs tests/cutie-binary-controller-router.test.cjs tests/cutie-binary-debug.test.cjs tests/cutie-debug-report.test.cjs
